Package glide.api.models

Class BaseBatch<T extends BaseBatch<T>>

  • java.lang.Object
    • glide.api.models.BaseBatch<T>
  • Type Parameters:
    T - child typing for chaining method calls.
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    Batch, ClusterBatch
    public abstract class BaseBatch<T extends BaseBatch<T>>
extends java.lang.Object
    Base class encompassing shared commands for both standalone and cluster server installations. Batches allow the execution of a group of commands in a single step.

    Batch Response: An array of command responses is returned by the client exec command, in the order they were given. Each element in the array represents a command given to the batch. The response for each command depends on the executed Valkey command. Specific response types are documented alongside each method.

    isAtomic: Determines whether the batch is atomic or non-atomic. If true, the batch will be executed as an atomic transaction. If false, the batch will be executed as a non-atomic pipeline.

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field Description
      protected boolean binaryOutput
      Flag whether batch commands may return binary data.
      If set to true, all commands in this batch return GlideString instead of String.
      protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Batch.Builder protobufBatch
      Command class to send a single request to Valkey.

      <ArgType> T bitop​(@NonNull BitwiseOperation bitwiseOperation, ArgType destination, @NonNull ArgType[] keys)

      <ArgType> T bitpos​(ArgType key, long bit)
      Returns the position of the first bit matching the given bit value.
      <ArgType> T bitpos​(ArgType key, long bit, long start)
      Returns the position of the first bit matching the given bit value.
      <ArgType> T bitpos​(ArgType key, long bit, long start, long end)

      <ArgType> T blmove​(ArgType source, ArgType destination, @NonNull ListDirection whereFrom, @NonNull ListDirection whereTo, double timeout)
      Blocks the connection until it atomically pops and removes the left/right-most element to the list stored at source depending on whereFrom, and pushes the element at the first/last element of the list stored at destination depending on whereFrom.
      BLMove is the blocking variant of #lmove(ArgType, ArgType, ListDirection, ListDirection).
      <ArgType> T blmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ListDirection direction, double timeout)
      Blocks the connection until it pops one element from the first non-empty list from the provided keys.
      <ArgType> T blmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ListDirection direction, @NonNull java.lang.Long count, double timeout)
      Blocks the connection until it pops one or more elements from the first non-empty list from the provided keys.
      <ArgType> T blpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, double timeout)
      Pops an element from the head of the first list that is non-empty, with the given keys being checked in the order that they are given.
      Blocks the connection when there are no elements to pop from any of the given lists.
      <ArgType> T brpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, double timeout)
      Pops an element from the tail of the first list that is non-empty, with the given keys being checked in the order that they are given.
      Blocks the connection when there are no elements to pop from any of the given lists.
      protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command buildCommand​(CommandRequestOuterClass.RequestType requestType)
      Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command object for given command and arguments.
      protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command buildCommand​(CommandRequestOuterClass.RequestType requestType, CommandRequestOuterClass.Command.ArgsArray args)
      Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command object for given command and arguments.
      protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command buildCommand​(CommandRequestOuterClass.RequestType requestType, ArgsBuilder argsBuilder)
      Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command object for given command and arguments.
      <ArgType> T bzmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ScoreFilter modifier, double timeout)
      Blocks the connection until it pops and returns a member-score pair from the first non-empty sorted set, with the given keys being checked in the order they are provided.
      BZMPOP is the blocking variant of #zmpop(ArgType[], ScoreFilter).
      <ArgType> T bzmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ScoreFilter modifier, double timeout, long count)
      Blocks the connection until it pops and returns multiple member-score pairs from the first non-empty sorted set, with the given keys being checked in the order they are provided.
      BZMPOP is the blocking variant of #zmpop(ArgType[], ScoreFilter, long).
      <ArgType> T bzpopmax​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, double timeout)
      Blocks the connection until it removes and returns a member with the highest score from the sorted sets stored at the specified keys.
      <ArgType> T bzpopmin​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, double timeout)
      Blocks the connection until it removes and returns a member with the lowest score from the sorted sets stored at the specified keys.

      <ArgType> T geoadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​GeospatialData> membersToGeospatialData)
      Adds geospatial members with their positions to the specified sorted set stored at key .
      If a member is already a part of the sorted set, its position is updated.
      To perform a geoadd operation while specifying optional parameters, use #geoadd(ArgType, Map, GeoAddOptions).
      <ArgType> T geoadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​GeospatialData> membersToGeospatialData, @NonNull GeoAddOptions options)
      Adds geospatial members with their positions to the specified sorted set stored at key .
      If a member is already a part of the sorted set, its position is updated.
      <ArgType> T geodist​(ArgType key, ArgType member1, ArgType member2)
      Returns the distance between member1 and member2 saved in the geospatial index stored at key.

      Returns the distance between member1 and member2 saved in the geospatial index stored at key.
      <ArgType> T geohash​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Returns the GeoHash strings representing the positions of all the specified members in the sorted set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T geopos​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Returns the positions (longitude,latitude) of all the specified members of the geospatial index represented by the sorted set at key.

      <ArgType> T hgetex​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] fields, @NonNull HGetExOptions options)
      Gets the values of the specified fields from the hash stored at key and optionally sets their expiration.
      If a field does not exist in the hash, a null value is returned for that field.

      Increments the number stored at field in the hash stored at key by increment.

      Increments the string representing a floating point number stored at field in the hash stored at key by increment.
      <ArgType> T hkeys​(ArgType key)
      Returns all field names in the hash stored at key.
      <ArgType> T hlen​(ArgType key)
      Returns the number of fields contained in the hash stored at key.
      <ArgType> T hmget​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
      Returns the values associated with the specified fields in the hash stored at key.
      <ArgType> T hpersist​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
      Removes the expiration time for each specified field, turning the field from volatile (a field with expiration time) to persistent (a field that will never expire as no expiration time is associated).
      <ArgType> T hpexpire​(ArgType key, long milliseconds, @NonNull ArgType[] fields, @NonNull HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options)
      Sets expiration time for hash fields, in milliseconds.

      Sets expiration time for hash fields, using an absolute Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
      <ArgType> T hpexpiretime​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
      Returns the absolute Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) at which the given hash fields will expire.
      <ArgType> T hpttl​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
      Returns the remaining time to live of hash fields that have a timeout, in milliseconds.
      <ArgType> T hrandfield​(ArgType key)
      Returns a random field name from the hash value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T hrandfieldWithCount​(ArgType key, long count)
      Retrieves up to count random field names from the hash value stored at key .
      <ArgType> T hrandfieldWithCountWithValues​(ArgType key, long count)
      Retrieves up to count random field names along with their values from the hash value stored at key.

      <ArgType> T hsetnx​(ArgType key, ArgType field, ArgType value)
      Sets field in the hash stored at key to value, only if field does not yet exist.
      If key does not exist, a new key holding a hash is created.
      If field already exists, this operation has no effect.

      Flag whether batch commands may return binary data.

      <ArgType> T lcs​(ArgType key1, ArgType key2)
      Returns all the longest common subsequences combined between strings stored at key1 and key2.
      <ArgType> T lcsIdx​(ArgType key1, ArgType key2)
      Returns the indices and length of the longest common subsequence between strings stored at key1 and key2.
      <ArgType> T lcsIdx​(ArgType key1, ArgType key2, long minMatchLen)
      Returns the indices and the total length of all the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
      <ArgType> T lcsIdxWithMatchLen​(ArgType key1, ArgType key2)
      Returns the indices and lengths of the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
      <ArgType> T lcsIdxWithMatchLen​(ArgType key1, ArgType key2, long minMatchLen)
      Returns the indices and lengths of the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
      <ArgType> T lcsLen​(ArgType key1, ArgType key2)
      Returns the total length of all the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
      <ArgType> T lindex​(ArgType key, long index)
      Returns the element at index from the list stored at key.
      The index is zero-based, so 0 means the first element, 1 the second element and so on.
      <ArgType> T linsert​(ArgType key, @NonNull LInsertOptions.InsertPosition position, ArgType pivot, ArgType element)
      Inserts element in the list at key either before or after the pivot.
      <ArgType> T llen​(ArgType key)
      Returns the length of the list stored at key.
      <ArgType> T lmove​(ArgType source, ArgType destination, @NonNull ListDirection whereFrom, @NonNull ListDirection whereTo)
      Atomically pops and removes the left/right-most element to the list stored at source depending on whereFrom, and pushes the element at the first/last element of the list stored at destination depending on whereFrom.
      <ArgType> T lmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ListDirection direction)
      Pops one element from the first non-empty list from the provided keys.
      <ArgType> T lmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ListDirection direction, @NonNull java.lang.Long count)
      Pops one or more elements from the first non-empty list from the provided keys .

      <ArgType> T lpop​(ArgType key)
      Removes and returns the first elements of the list stored at key.
      <ArgType> T lpopCount​(ArgType key, long count)
      Removes and returns up to count elements of the list stored at key, depending on the list's length.
      <ArgType> T lpos​(ArgType key, ArgType element)
      Returns the index of the first occurrence of element inside the list specified by key.
      <ArgType> T lpos​(ArgType key, ArgType element, @NonNull LPosOptions options)
      Returns the index of an occurrence of element within a list based on the given options.
      <ArgType> T lposCount​(ArgType key, ArgType element, long count)
      Returns an array of indices of matching elements within a list.
      <ArgType> T lposCount​(ArgType key, ArgType element, long count, @NonNull LPosOptions options)
      Returns an array of indices of matching elements within a list based on the given options.
      <ArgType> T lpush​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] elements)
      Inserts all the specified values at the head of the list stored at key.
      <ArgType> T lpushx​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] elements)
      Inserts all the specified values at the head of the list stored at key, only if key exists and holds a list.
      <ArgType> T lrange​(ArgType key, long start, long end)
      Returns the specified elements of the list stored at key.
      The offsets start and end are zero-based indexes, with 0 being the first element of the list, 1 being the next element and so on.
      <ArgType> T lrem​(ArgType key, long count, ArgType element)
      Removes the first count occurrences of elements equal to element from the list stored at key.
      <ArgType> T lset​(ArgType key, long index, ArgType element)
      Sets the list element at index to element.
      The index is zero-based, so 0 means the first element, 1 the second element and so on.
      <ArgType> T ltrim​(ArgType key, long start, long end)
      Trims an existing list so that it will contain only the specified range of elements specified.
      <ArgType> T mget​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Retrieves the values of multiple keys.
      <ArgType> T move​(ArgType key, long dbIndex)
      Move key from the currently selected database to the database specified by dbIndex.
      T mset​(@NonNull java.util.Map<?,​?> keyValueMap)
      Sets multiple keys to multiple values in a single operation.
      T msetnx​(@NonNull java.util.Map<?,​?> keyValueMap)
      Sets multiple keys to multiple values in a single operation.
      <ArgType> T objectEncoding​(ArgType key)
      Returns the internal encoding for the server object stored at key.
      <ArgType> T objectFreq​(ArgType key)
      Returns the logarithmic access frequency counter of a server object stored at key.
      <ArgType> T objectIdletime​(ArgType key)
      Returns the time in seconds since the last access to the value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T objectRefcount​(ArgType key)
      Returns the reference count of the object stored at key.
      <ArgType> T persist​(ArgType key)
      Removes the existing timeout on key, turning the key from volatile (a key with an expire set) to persistent (a key that will never expire as no timeout is associated).
      <ArgType> T pexpire​(ArgType key, long milliseconds)
      Sets a timeout on key in milliseconds.
      <ArgType> T pexpire​(ArgType key, long milliseconds, @NonNull ExpireOptions expireOptions)
      Sets a timeout on key in milliseconds.
      <ArgType> T pexpireAt​(ArgType key, long unixMilliseconds)
      Sets a timeout on key.
      <ArgType> T pexpireAt​(ArgType key, long unixMilliseconds, @NonNull ExpireOptions expireOptions)
      Sets a timeout on key.
      <ArgType> T pexpiretime​(ArgType key)
      Returns the absolute Unix timestamp (since January 1, 1970) at which the given key will expire, in milliseconds.
      <ArgType> T pfadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] elements)
      Adds all elements to the HyperLogLog data structure stored at the specified key.
      <ArgType> T pfcount​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Estimates the cardinality of the data stored in a HyperLogLog structure for a single key or calculates the combined cardinality of multiple keys by merging their HyperLogLogs temporarily.
      <ArgType> T pfmerge​(ArgType destination, @NonNull ArgType[] sourceKeys)
      Merges multiple HyperLogLog values into a unique value.
      If the destination variable exists, it is treated as one of the source HyperLogLog data sets, otherwise a new HyperLogLog is created.
      T ping()
      Pings the server.
      <ArgType> T ping​(ArgType msg)
      Pings the server.
      <ArgType> T pttl​(ArgType key)
      Returns the remaining time to live of key that has a timeout, in milliseconds.
      <ArgType> T publish​(ArgType message, ArgType channel)
      Publishes message on pubsub channel.
      T pubsubChannels()
      Lists the currently active channels.
      <ArgType> T pubsubChannels​(ArgType pattern)
      Lists the currently active channels.
      T pubsubNumPat()
      Returns the number of unique patterns that are subscribed to by clients.
      <ArgType> T pubsubNumSub​(@NonNull ArgType[] channels)
      Returns the number of subscribers (exclusive of clients subscribed to patterns) for the specified channels.
      T randomKey()
      Returns a random key from the currently selected database.
      <ArgType> T rename​(ArgType key, ArgType newKey)
      Renames key to newKey.
      If newKey already exists it is overwritten.
      <ArgType> T renamenx​(ArgType key, ArgType newKey)
      Renames key to newKey if newKey does not yet exist.
      <ArgType> T restore​(ArgType key, long ttl, @lombok.NonNull byte[] value)
      Create a key associated with a value that is obtained by deserializing the provided serialized value (obtained via dump(ArgType)).
      <ArgType> T restore​(ArgType key, long ttl, @lombok.NonNull byte[] value, @NonNull RestoreOptions restoreOptions)
      Create a key associated with a value that is obtained by deserializing the provided serialized value (obtained via dump(ArgType)).
      <ArgType> T rpop​(ArgType key)
      Removes and returns the last elements of the list stored at key.
      The command pops a single element from the end of the list.
      <ArgType> T rpopCount​(ArgType key, long count)
      Removes and returns up to count elements from the list stored at key, depending on the list's length.
      <ArgType> T rpush​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] elements)
      Inserts all the specified values at the tail of the list stored at key.
      elements are inserted one after the other to the tail of the list, from the leftmost element to the rightmost element.
      <ArgType> T rpushx​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] elements)
      Inserts all the specified values at the tail of the list stored at key, only if key exists and holds a list.
      <ArgType> T sadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Adds specified members to the set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T scard​(ArgType key)
      Retrieves the set cardinality (number of elements) of the set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T sdiff​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Computes the difference between the first set and all the successive sets in keys.
      <ArgType> T sdiffstore​(ArgType destination, @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Stores the difference between the first set and all the successive sets in keys into a new set at destination.
      <ArgType> T set​(ArgType key, ArgType value)
      Sets the given key with the given value.
      <ArgType> T set​(ArgType key, ArgType value, @NonNull SetOptions options)
      Sets the given key with the given value.
      <ArgType> T setbit​(ArgType key, long offset, long value)
      Sets or clears the bit at offset in the string value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T setrange​(ArgType key, int offset, ArgType value)
      Overwrites part of the string stored at key, starting at the specified offset, for the entire length of value.
      If the offset is larger than the current length of the string at key, the string is padded with zero bytes to make offset fit.
      <ArgType> T sinter​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Gets the intersection of all the given sets.
      <ArgType> T sintercard​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Gets the cardinality of the intersection of all the given sets.
      <ArgType> T sintercard​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, long limit)
      Gets the cardinality of the intersection of all the given sets.
      <ArgType> T sinterstore​(ArgType destination, @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Stores the members of the intersection of all given sets specified by keys into a new set at destination.
      <ArgType> T sismember​(ArgType key, ArgType member)
      Returns if member is a member of the set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T smembers​(ArgType key)
      Retrieves all the members of the set value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T smismember​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Checks whether each member is contained in the members of the set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T smove​(ArgType source, ArgType destination, ArgType member)
      Moves member from the set at source to the set at destination , removing it from the source set.
      <ArgType> T sort​(ArgType key)
      Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result.
      <ArgType> T sort​(ArgType key, @NonNull SortOptions sortOptions)
      Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result.
      <ArgType> T sortReadOnly​(ArgType key)
      Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result.
      <ArgType> T sortReadOnly​(ArgType key, @NonNull SortOptions sortOptions)
      Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result.
      <ArgType> T sortStore​(ArgType key, ArgType destination)
      Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and stores the result in destination.
      <ArgType> T sortStore​(ArgType key, ArgType destination, @NonNull SortOptions sortOptions)
      Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and stores the result in destination.
      <ArgType> T spop​(ArgType key)
      Removes and returns one random member from the set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T spopCount​(ArgType key, long count)
      Removes and returns up to count random members from the set stored at key , depending on the set's length.
      <ArgType> T srandmember​(ArgType key)
      Returns a random element from the set value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T srandmember​(ArgType key, long count)
      Returns random elements from the set value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T srem​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Removes specified members from the set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T sscan​(ArgType key, ArgType cursor)
      Iterates incrementally over a set.
      <ArgType> T sscan​(ArgType key, ArgType cursor, @NonNull SScanOptions sScanOptions)
      Iterates incrementally over a set.
      <ArgType> T strlen​(ArgType key)
      Returns the length of the string value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T sunion​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Gets the union of all the given sets.
      <ArgType> T sunionstore​(ArgType destination, @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Stores the members of the union of all given sets specified by keys into a new set at destination.
      T time()
      Returns the server time.
      <ArgType> T touch​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Updates the last access time of specified keys.
      <ArgType> T ttl​(ArgType key)
      Returns the remaining time to live of key that has a timeout.
      <ArgType> T type​(ArgType key)
      Returns the string representation of the type of the value stored at key.
      <ArgType> T unlink​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Unlinks (deletes) multiple keys from the database.
      T wait​(long numReplicas, long timeout)
      Returns the number of replicas that acknowledged the write commands sent by the current client before this command, both in the case where the specified number of replicas are reached, or when the timeout is reached.
      T withBinaryOutput()
      Sets binaryOutput to true.
      <ArgType> T xack​(ArgType key, ArgType group, @NonNull ArgType[] ids)
      Returns the number of messages that were successfully acknowledged by the consumer group member of a stream.
      <ArgType> T xadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[][] values)
      Adds an entry to the specified stream stored at key.
      If the key doesn't exist, the stream is created.
      <ArgType> T xadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[][] values, @NonNull StreamAddOptions options)
      Adds an entry to the specified stream stored at key.
      If the key doesn't exist, the stream is created.
      <ArgType> T xadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> values)
      Adds an entry to the specified stream stored at key.
      If the key doesn't exist, the stream is created.
      <ArgType> T xadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> values, @NonNull StreamAddOptions options)
      Adds an entry to the specified stream stored at key.
      If the key doesn't exist, the stream is created.
      <ArgType> T xautoclaim​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, ArgType start)
      Transfers ownership of pending stream entries that match the specified criteria.
      <ArgType> T xautoclaim​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, ArgType start, long count)
      Transfers ownership of pending stream entries that match the specified criteria.
      <ArgType> T xautoclaimJustId​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, ArgType start)
      Transfers ownership of pending stream entries that match the specified criteria.
      <ArgType> T xautoclaimJustId​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, ArgType start, long count)
      Transfers ownership of pending stream entries that match the specified criteria.
      <ArgType> T xclaim​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, @NonNull ArgType[] ids)
      Changes the ownership of a pending message.
      <ArgType> T xclaim​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, @NonNull ArgType[] ids, @NonNull StreamClaimOptions options)
      Changes the ownership of a pending message.
      <ArgType> T xclaimJustId​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, @NonNull ArgType[] ids)
      Changes the ownership of a pending message.
      <ArgType> T xclaimJustId​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, long minIdleTime, @NonNull ArgType[] ids, @NonNull StreamClaimOptions options)
      Changes the ownership of a pending message.
      <ArgType> T xdel​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] ids)
      Removes the specified entries by id from a stream, and returns the number of entries deleted.
      <ArgType> T xgroupCreate​(ArgType key, ArgType groupName, ArgType id)
      Creates a new consumer group uniquely identified by groupname for the stream stored at key.
      <ArgType> T xgroupCreate​(ArgType key, ArgType groupName, ArgType id, @NonNull StreamGroupOptions options)
      Creates a new consumer group uniquely identified by groupname for the stream stored at key.
      <ArgType> T xgroupCreateConsumer​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer)
      Creates a consumer named consumer in the consumer group group for the stream stored at key.
      <ArgType> T xgroupDelConsumer​(ArgType key, ArgType group, ArgType consumer)
      Deletes a consumer named consumer in the consumer group group.
      <ArgType> T xgroupDestroy​(ArgType key, ArgType groupName)
      Destroys the consumer group groupName for the stream stored at key.
      <ArgType> T xgroupSetId​(ArgType key, ArgType groupName, ArgType id)
      Sets the last delivered ID for a consumer group.
      <ArgType> T xgroupSetId​(ArgType key, ArgType groupName, ArgType id, long entriesRead)
      Sets the last delivered ID for a consumer group.
      <ArgType> T xinfoConsumers​(ArgType key, ArgType groupName)
      Returns the list of all consumers and their attributes for the given consumer group of the stream stored at key.
      <ArgType> T xinfoGroups​(ArgType key)
      Returns the list of all consumer groups and their attributes for the stream stored at key .
      <ArgType> T xinfoStream​(ArgType key)
      Returns information about the stream stored at key key.
      To get more detailed information use #xinfoStreamFull(ArgType) or #xinfoStreamFull(ArgType, int).
      <ArgType> T xinfoStreamFull​(ArgType key)
      Returns verbose information about the stream stored at key key.
      The output is limited by first 10 PEL entries.
      <ArgType> T xinfoStreamFull​(ArgType key, int count)
      Returns verbose information about the stream stored at key key.
      The output is limited by first 10 PEL entries.
      <ArgType> T xlen​(ArgType key)
      Returns the number of entries in the stream stored at key.
      <ArgType> T xpending​(ArgType key, ArgType group)
      Returns stream message summary information for pending messages matching a given range of IDs.
      <ArgType> T xpending​(ArgType key, ArgType group, @NonNull StreamRange start, @NonNull StreamRange end, long count)
      Returns an extended form of stream message information for pending messages matching a given range of IDs.
      <ArgType> T xpending​(ArgType key, ArgType group, @NonNull StreamRange start, @NonNull StreamRange end, long count, @NonNull StreamPendingOptions options)
      Returns an extended form of stream message information for pending messages matching a given range of IDs.
      <ArgType> T xrange​(ArgType key, @NonNull StreamRange start, @NonNull StreamRange end)
      Returns stream entries matching a given range of IDs.
      <ArgType> T xrange​(ArgType key, @NonNull StreamRange start, @NonNull StreamRange end, long count)
      Returns stream entries matching a given range of IDs.
      <ArgType> T xread​(@NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> keysAndIds)
      Reads entries from the given streams.
      <ArgType> T xread​(@NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> keysAndIds, @NonNull StreamReadOptions options)
      Reads entries from the given streams.
      <ArgType> T xreadgroup​(@NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> keysAndIds, ArgType group, ArgType consumer)
      Reads entries from the given streams owned by a consumer group.
      <ArgType> T xreadgroup​(@NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> keysAndIds, ArgType group, ArgType consumer, @NonNull StreamReadGroupOptions options)
      Reads entries from the given streams owned by a consumer group.
      <ArgType> T xrevrange​(ArgType key, @NonNull StreamRange end, @NonNull StreamRange start)
      Returns stream entries matching a given range of IDs in reverse order.
      Equivalent to #xrange(ArgType, StreamRange, StreamRange) but returns the entries in reverse order.
      <ArgType> T xrevrange​(ArgType key, @NonNull StreamRange end, @NonNull StreamRange start, long count)
      Returns stream entries matching a given range of IDs in reverse order.
      Equivalent to #xrange(ArgType, StreamRange, StreamRange, long) but returns the entries in reverse order.
      <ArgType> T xtrim​(ArgType key, @NonNull StreamTrimOptions options)
      Trims the stream by evicting older entries.
      <ArgType> T zadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​java.lang.Double> membersScoresMap)
      Adds members with their scores to the sorted set stored at key.
      If a member is already a part of the sorted set, its score is updated.
      <ArgType> T zadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​java.lang.Double> membersScoresMap, boolean changed)
      Adds members with their scores to the sorted set stored at key.
      If a member is already a part of the sorted set, its score is updated.
      <ArgType> T zadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​java.lang.Double> membersScoresMap, @NonNull ZAddOptions options)
      Adds members with their scores to the sorted set stored at key.
      If a member is already a part of the sorted set, its score is updated.
      <ArgType> T zadd​(ArgType key, @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​java.lang.Double> membersScoresMap, @NonNull ZAddOptions options, boolean changed)
      Adds members with their scores to the sorted set stored at key.
      If a member is already a part of the sorted set, its score is updated.
      <ArgType> T zaddIncr​(ArgType key, ArgType member, double increment)
      Increments the score of member in the sorted set stored at key by increment .
      If member does not exist in the sorted set, it is added with increment as its score (as if its previous score was 0.0).
      If key does not exist, a new sorted set with the specified member as its sole member is created.
      <ArgType> T zaddIncr​(ArgType key, ArgType member, double increment, @NonNull ZAddOptions options)
      Increments the score of member in the sorted set stored at key by increment .
      If member does not exist in the sorted set, it is added with increment as its score (as if its previous score was 0.0).
      If key does not exist, a new sorted set with the specified member as its sole member is created.
      zaddIncr with empty option acts as #zincrby(ArgType, double, ArgType).
      <ArgType> T zcard​(ArgType key)
      Returns the cardinality (number of elements) of the sorted set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T zcount​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.ScoreRange minScore, @NonNull RangeOptions.ScoreRange maxScore)
      Returns the number of members in the sorted set stored at key with scores between minScore and maxScore.
      <ArgType> T zdiff​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Returns the difference between the first sorted set and all the successive sorted sets.
      To get the elements with their scores, see zdiffWithScores(ArgType[]).
      <ArgType> T zdiffstore​(ArgType destination, @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Calculates the difference between the first sorted set and all the successive sorted sets at keys and stores the difference as a sorted set to destination, overwriting it if it already exists.
      <ArgType> T zdiffWithScores​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Returns the difference between the first sorted set and all the successive sorted sets.
      <ArgType> T zincrby​(ArgType key, double increment, ArgType member)
      Increments the score of member in the sorted set stored at key by increment.
      If member does not exist in the sorted set, it is added with increment as its score.
      T zinter​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeyArray keys)
      Returns the intersection of members from sorted sets specified by the given keys.
      T zinter​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeyArrayBinary keys)
      Returns the intersection of members from sorted sets specified by the given keys.
      <ArgType> T zintercard​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys)
      Returns the cardinality of the intersection of the sorted sets specified by keys.
      <ArgType> T zintercard​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, long limit)
      Returns the cardinality of the intersection of the sorted sets specified by keys.
      T zinterstore​(@NonNull GlideString destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Computes the intersection of sorted sets given by the specified KeysOrWeightedKeys , and stores the result in destination.
      T zinterstore​(@NonNull GlideString destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Computes the intersection of sorted sets given by the specified keysOrWeightedKeys , and stores the result in destination.
      T zinterstore​(@NonNull java.lang.String destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Computes the intersection of sorted sets given by the specified KeysOrWeightedKeys , and stores the result in destination.
      T zinterstore​(@NonNull java.lang.String destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Computes the intersection of sorted sets given by the specified keysOrWeightedKeys , and stores the result in destination.
      T zinterWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Returns the intersection of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      T zinterWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Returns the intersection of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      T zinterWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Returns the intersection of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      T zinterWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Returns the intersection of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      <ArgType> T zlexcount​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.LexRange minLex, @NonNull RangeOptions.LexRange maxLex)
      Returns the number of members in the sorted set stored at key with scores between minLex and maxLex.
      <ArgType> T zmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ScoreFilter modifier)
      Pops a member-score pair from the first non-empty sorted set, with the given keys being checked in the order they are provided.
      <ArgType> T zmpop​(@NonNull ArgType[] keys, @NonNull ScoreFilter modifier, long count)
      Pops multiple member-score pairs from the first non-empty sorted set, with the given keys being checked in the order they are provided.
      <ArgType> T zmscore​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Returns the scores associated with the specified members in the sorted set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T zpopmax​(ArgType key)
      Removes and returns the member with the highest score from the sorted set stored at the specified key.
      <ArgType> T zpopmax​(ArgType key, long count)
      Removes and returns up to count members with the highest scores from the sorted set stored at the specified key.
      <ArgType> T zpopmin​(ArgType key)
      Removes and returns the member with the lowest score from the sorted set stored at the specified key.
      <ArgType> T zpopmin​(ArgType key, long count)
      Removes and returns up to count members with the lowest scores from the sorted set stored at the specified key.
      <ArgType> T zrandmember​(ArgType key)
      Returns a random element from the sorted set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T zrandmemberWithCount​(ArgType key, long count)
      Retrieves random elements from the sorted set stored at key.
      <ArgType> T zrandmemberWithCountWithScores​(ArgType key, long count)
      Retrieves random elements along with their scores from the sorted set stored at key .
      <ArgType> T zrange​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.RangeQuery rangeQuery)
      Returns the specified range of elements in the sorted set stored at key.
      ZRANGE can perform different types of range queries: by index (rank), by the score, or by lexicographical order.
      To get the elements with their scores, see zrangeWithScores(ArgType, glide.api.models.commands.RangeOptions.ScoredRangeQuery, boolean).
      <ArgType> T zrange​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.RangeQuery rangeQuery, boolean reverse)
      Returns the specified range of elements in the sorted set stored at key.
      ZRANGE can perform different types of range queries: by index (rank), by the score, or by lexicographical order.
      To get the elements with their scores, see zrangeWithScores(ArgType, glide.api.models.commands.RangeOptions.ScoredRangeQuery, boolean).
      <ArgType> T zrangestore​(ArgType destination, ArgType source, @NonNull RangeOptions.RangeQuery rangeQuery)
      Stores a specified range of elements from the sorted set at source, into a new sorted set at destination.
      <ArgType> T zrangestore​(ArgType destination, ArgType source, @NonNull RangeOptions.RangeQuery rangeQuery, boolean reverse)
      Stores a specified range of elements from the sorted set at source, into a new sorted set at destination.
      <ArgType> T zrangeWithScores​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.ScoredRangeQuery rangeQuery)
      Returns the specified range of elements with their scores in the sorted set stored at key .
      <ArgType> T zrangeWithScores​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.ScoredRangeQuery rangeQuery, boolean reverse)
      Returns the specified range of elements with their scores in the sorted set stored at key .
      <ArgType> T zrank​(ArgType key, ArgType member)
      Returns the rank of member in the sorted set stored at key, with scores ordered from low to high, starting from 0.
      To get the rank of member with its score, see zrankWithScore(ArgType, ArgType).
      <ArgType> T zrankWithScore​(ArgType key, ArgType member)
      Returns the rank of member in the sorted set stored at key with its score, where scores are ordered from the lowest to highest, starting from 0.
      <ArgType> T zrem​(ArgType key, @NonNull ArgType[] members)
      Removes the specified members from the sorted set stored at key.
      Specified members that are not a member of this set are ignored.
      <ArgType> T zremrangebylex​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.LexRange minLex, @NonNull RangeOptions.LexRange maxLex)
      Removes all elements in the sorted set stored at key with a lexicographical order between minLex and maxLex.
      <ArgType> T zremrangebyrank​(ArgType key, long start, long end)
      Removes all elements in the sorted set stored at key with rank between start and end.
      <ArgType> T zremrangebyscore​(ArgType key, @NonNull RangeOptions.ScoreRange minScore, @NonNull RangeOptions.ScoreRange maxScore)
      Removes all elements in the sorted set stored at key with a score between minScore and maxScore.
      <ArgType> T zrevrank​(ArgType key, ArgType member)
      Returns the rank of member in the sorted set stored at key, where scores are ordered from the highest to lowest, starting from 0.
      To get the rank of member with its score, see zrevrankWithScore(ArgType, ArgType).
      <ArgType> T zrevrankWithScore​(ArgType key, ArgType member)
      Returns the rank of member in the sorted set stored at key with its score, where scores are ordered from the highest to lowest, starting from 0.
      <ArgType> T zscan​(ArgType key, ArgType cursor)
      Iterates incrementally over a sorted set.
      <ArgType> T zscan​(ArgType key, ArgType cursor, @NonNull ZScanOptions zScanOptions)
      Iterates incrementally over a sorted set.
      <ArgType> T zscore​(ArgType key, ArgType member)
      Returns the score of member in the sorted set stored at key.
      T zunion​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeyArray keys)
      Returns the union of members from sorted sets specified by the given keys.
      To get the elements with their scores, see zunionWithScores(glide.api.models.commands.WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys, glide.api.models.commands.WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate).
      T zunion​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeyArrayBinary keys)
      Returns the union of members from sorted sets specified by the given keys.
      To get the elements with their scores, see zunionWithScores(glide.api.models.commands.WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys, glide.api.models.commands.WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate).
      T zunionstore​(@NonNull GlideString destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Computes the union of sorted sets given by the specified KeysOrWeightedKeys, and stores the result in destination.
      T zunionstore​(@NonNull GlideString destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Computes the union of sorted sets given by the specified KeysOrWeightedKeys, and stores the result in destination.
      T zunionstore​(@NonNull java.lang.String destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Computes the union of sorted sets given by the specified KeysOrWeightedKeys, and stores the result in destination.
      T zunionstore​(@NonNull java.lang.String destination, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Computes the union of sorted sets given by the specified KeysOrWeightedKeys, and stores the result in destination.
      T zunionWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Returns the union of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      To perform a zunion operation while specifying aggregation settings, use zunionWithScores(KeysOrWeightedKeys, Aggregate).
      T zunionWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys)
      Returns the union of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      To perform a zunion operation while specifying aggregation settings, use zunionWithScores(KeysOrWeightedKeys, Aggregate).
      T zunionWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeysBinary keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Returns the union of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.
      T zunionWithScores​(@NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.KeysOrWeightedKeys keysOrWeightedKeys, @NonNull WeightAggregateOptions.Aggregate aggregate)
      Returns the union of members and their scores from sorted sets specified by the given keysOrWeightedKeys.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • protobufBatch

        protected final CommandRequestOuterClass.Batch.Builder protobufBatch
        Command class to send a single request to Valkey.

      • binaryOutput

        protected boolean binaryOutput
        Flag whether batch commands may return binary data.
        If set to true, all commands in this batch return GlideString instead of String.

    • Constructor Detail

      • BaseBatch

        protected BaseBatch​(boolean isAtomic)

    • Method Detail

      • withBinaryOutput

        public T withBinaryOutput()
        Sets binaryOutput to true.

      • getThis

        protected abstract T getThis()

      • customCommand

        public <ArgType> T customCommand​(ArgType[] args)
        Executes a single command, without checking inputs. Every part of the command, including subcommands, should be added as a separate value in args.
        Parameters:
        args - Arguments for the custom command.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The returned value for the custom command.

      • echo

        public <ArgType> T echo​(@NonNull
                        ArgType message)
        Echoes the provided message back.
        Parameters:
        message - The message to be echoed back.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The provided message.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • ping

        public T ping()
        Pings the server.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A response from the server with a String.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • ping

        public <ArgType> T ping​(@NonNull
                        ArgType msg)
        Pings the server.
        Parameters:
        msg - The ping argument that will be returned.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A response from the server with a String.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • info

        public T info()
        Gets information and statistics about the server using the InfoOptions.Section.DEFAULT option.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A String with server info.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • info

        public T info​(@NonNull
              @NonNull InfoOptions.Section[] sections)
        Gets information and statistics about the server.
        Starting from server version 7, command supports multiple section arguments.
        Parameters:
        sections - A list of InfoOptions.Section values specifying which sections of information to retrieve. When no parameter is provided, the InfoOptions.Section.DEFAULT option is assumed.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A String containing the requested InfoOptions.Sections.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • del

        public <ArgType> T del​(@NonNull
                       @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
        Removes the specified keys from the database. A key is ignored if it does not exist.
        Parameters:
        keys - The keys we wanted to remove.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of keys that were removed.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • get

        public <ArgType> T get​(@NonNull
                       ArgType key)
        Gets the value associated with the given key, or null if no such key exists.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to retrieve from the database.
        Returns:
        Command Response - If key exists, returns the value of key as a String. Otherwise, return null.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • getdel

        public <ArgType> T getdel​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key)
        Gets a string value associated with the given key and deletes the key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to retrieve from the database.
        Returns:
        Command Response - If key exists, returns the value of key. Otherwise, return null.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • getex

        public <ArgType> T getex​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key)
        Gets the value associated with the given key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to retrieve from the database.
        Returns:
        Command Response - If key exists, return the value of the key. Otherwise, return null.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • getex

        public <ArgType> T getex​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull GetExOptions options)
        Gets the value associated with the given key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to retrieve from the database.
        options - The GetExOptions options.
        Returns:
        Command Response - If key exists, return the value of the key. Otherwise, return null.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • set

        public <ArgType> T set​(@NonNull
                       ArgType key,
                       @NonNull
                       ArgType value)
        Sets the given key with the given value.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to store.
        value - The value to store with the given key.
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • set

        public <ArgType> T set​(@NonNull
                       ArgType key,
                       @NonNull
                       ArgType value,
                       @NonNull
                       @NonNull SetOptions options)
        Sets the given key with the given value. Return value is dependent on the passed options.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to store.
        value - The value to store with the given key.
        options - The Set options.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A String or null response. The old value as a String if SetOptions.SetOptionsBuilder.returnOldValue(boolean) is set. Otherwise, if the value isn't set because of SetOptions.ConditionalSet.ONLY_IF_EXISTS or SetOptions.ConditionalSet.ONLY_IF_DOES_NOT_EXIST conditions, return null. Otherwise, return OK.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • append

        public <ArgType> T append​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType value)
        Appends a value to a key. If key does not exist it is created and set as an empty string, so APPEND will be similar to set(ArgType, ArgType) in this special case.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        value - The value to append.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The length of the string after appending the value.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • mget

        public <ArgType> T mget​(@NonNull
                        @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
        Retrieves the values of multiple keys.
        Parameters:
        keys - A list of keys to retrieve values for.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of values corresponding to the provided keys.
        If a keyis not found, its corresponding value in the list will be null .
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • mset

        public T mset​(@NonNull
              @NonNull java.util.Map<?,​?> keyValueMap)
        Sets multiple keys to multiple values in a single operation.
        Parameters:
        keyValueMap - A key-value map consisting of keys and their respective values to set.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A simple OK response.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • msetnx

        public T msetnx​(@NonNull
                @NonNull java.util.Map<?,​?> keyValueMap)
        Sets multiple keys to multiple values in a single operation. Performs no operation at all even if just a single key already exists.
        Parameters:
        keyValueMap - A key-value map consisting of keys and their respective values to set.
        Returns:
        Command Response - true if all keys were set, false if no key was set.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • move

        public <ArgType> T move​(ArgType key,
                        long dbIndex)
        Move key from the currently selected database to the database specified by dbIndex.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to move.
        dbIndex - The index of the database to move key to.
        Returns:
        Command Response - true if key was moved, or false if the key already exists in the destination database or does not exist in the source database.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • incr

        public <ArgType> T incr​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key)
        Increments the number stored at key by one. If key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to increment its value.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of key after the increment.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • incrBy

        public <ArgType> T incrBy​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long amount)
        Increments the number stored at key by amount. If key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to increment its value.
        amount - The amount to increment.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of key after the increment.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • incrByFloat

        public <ArgType> T incrByFloat​(@NonNull
                               ArgType key,
                               double amount)
        Increments the string representing a floating point number stored at key by amount. By using a negative increment value, the result is that the value stored at key is decremented. If key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to increment its value.
        amount - The amount to increment.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of key after the increment.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • decr

        public <ArgType> T decr​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key)
        Decrements the number stored at key by one. If key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to decrement its value.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of key after the decrement.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • decrBy

        public <ArgType> T decrBy​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long amount)
        Decrements the number stored at key by amount. If key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to decrement its value.
        amount - The amount to decrement.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of key after the decrement.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • strlen

        public <ArgType> T strlen​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key)
        Returns the length of the string value stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key to check its length.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The length of the string value stored at key.
        If key does not exist, it is treated as an empty string, and the command returns 0.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • setrange

        public <ArgType> T setrange​(@NonNull
                            ArgType key,
                            int offset,
                            @NonNull
                            ArgType value)
        Overwrites part of the string stored at key, starting at the specified offset, for the entire length of value.
        If the offset is larger than the current length of the string at key, the string is padded with zero bytes to make offset fit. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string to update.
        offset - The position in the string where value should be written.
        value - The string written with offset.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The length of the string stored at key after it was modified.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • getrange

        public <ArgType> T getrange​(@NonNull
                            ArgType key,
                            int start,
                            int end)
        Returns the substring of the string value stored at key, determined by the offsets start and end (both are inclusive). Negative offsets can be used in order to provide an offset starting from the end of the string. So -1 means the last character, -2 the penultimate and so forth. If key does not exist, an empty string is returned. If start or end are out of range, returns the substring within the valid range of the string.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        start - The starting offset.
        end - The ending offset.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A substring extracted from the value stored at key.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hget

        public <ArgType> T hget​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key,
                        @NonNull
                        ArgType field)
        Retrieves the value associated with field in the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        field - The field in the hash stored at key to retrieve from the database.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value associated with field, or null when field is not present in the hash or key does not exist.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hset

        public <ArgType> T hset​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key,
                        @NonNull
                        @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> fieldValueMap)
        Sets the specified fields to their respective values in the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fieldValueMap - A field-value map consisting of fields and their corresponding values to be set in the hash stored at the specified key.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of fields that were added.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hsetex

        public <ArgType> T hsetex​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull java.util.Map<ArgType,​ArgType> fieldValueMap,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull HSetExOptions options)
        Sets the specified fields to their respective values in the hash stored at key with optional expiration. If key does not exist, a new key holding a hash is created.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fieldValueMap - A field-value map consisting of fields and their corresponding values to be set in the hash stored at the specified key.
        options - The HSetExOptions for the command, including field conditional changes and expiry settings.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of fields that were added to the hash.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0.0.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 // Set fields with 60 second expiration, only if none exist
 HSetExOptions options = HSetExOptions.builder()
     .onlyIfNoneExist()
     .expiry(ExpirySet.Seconds(60L))
     .build();

 Map<String, String> fieldValueMap = Map.of("field1", "value1", "field2", "value2");
 batch.hsetex("myHash", fieldValueMap, options);

      • hgetex

        public <ArgType> T hgetex​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull ArgType[] fields,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull HGetExOptions options)
        Gets the values of the specified fields from the hash stored at key and optionally sets their expiration.
        If a field does not exist in the hash, a null value is returned for that field.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to retrieve from the hash.
        options - The HGetExOptions for the command, including expiry settings that support PERSIST but exclude KEEPTTL.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of values associated with the given fields, in the same order as they are requested. For every field that does not exist in the hash, a null value is returned.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0.0.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 // Get fields and set them to persist (no expiration)
 HGetExOptions options = HGetExOptions.builder()
     .expiry(HGetExExpiry.Persist())
     .build();

 String[] fields = {"field1", "field2"};
 batch.hgetex("myHash", fields, options);

      • hexpire

        public <ArgType> T hexpire​(@NonNull
                           ArgType key,
                           long seconds,
                           @NonNull
                           @NonNull ArgType[] fields,
                           @NonNull
                           @NonNull HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options)
        Sets expiration time for hash fields. HEXPIRE sets the expiration time in seconds for the specified fields of the hash stored at key. You can specify whether to set the expiration only if the field has no expiration, only if the field has an existing expiration, only if the new expiration is greater than the current one, or only if the new expiration is less than the current one.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        seconds - The expiration time in seconds.
        fields - The fields in the hash stored at key to set expiration for.
        options - The HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions for the command, supporting only expiration conditions (NX/XX/GT/LT).
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of Boolean values indicating the success of setting expiration for each field. true indicates that the expiration was successfully set, and false indicates that the condition was not met or the field does not exist.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 // Set expiration only if fields have no existing expiration
 HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options = HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions.builder()
     .onlyIfNoExpiry()
     .build();

 String[] fields = {"field1", "field2"};
 batch.hexpire("myHash", 300L, fields, options);

      • hpersist

        public <ArgType> T hpersist​(@NonNull
                            ArgType key,
                            @NonNull
                            @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Removes the expiration time for each specified field, turning the field from volatile (a field with expiration time) to persistent (a field that will never expire as no expiration time is associated).
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to remove expiration from.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of Boolean values, each corresponding to a field:
        • true if the expiration time was successfully removed from the field.
        • false if the field does not exist or does not have an expiration time.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hpexpire

        public <ArgType> T hpexpire​(@NonNull
                            ArgType key,
                            long milliseconds,
                            @NonNull
                            @NonNull ArgType[] fields,
                            @NonNull
                            @NonNull HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options)
        Sets expiration time for hash fields, in milliseconds. Creates the hash if it doesn't exist. If a field is already expired, it will be deleted rather than expired.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        milliseconds - The expiration time to set for the fields, in milliseconds.
        fields - The fields to set expiration for.
        options - The HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions for the command, supporting only expiration conditions (NX/XX/GT/LT).
        Returns:
        Command response - An array of Boolean values, each corresponding to a field:
        • true if the expiration time was successfully set for the field.
        • false if the field does not exist or the expiration time was not set due to the condition not being met.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 // Set expiration only if new expiration is greater than current
 HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options = HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions.builder()
     .onlyIfGreaterThanCurrent()
     .build();

 String[] fields = {"field1", "field2"};
 batch.hpexpire("myHash", 30000L, fields, options);

      • hexpireat

        public <ArgType> T hexpireat​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             long unixSeconds,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull ArgType[] fields,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options)
        Sets expiration time for hash fields, in seconds, using an absolute Unix timestamp. Creates the hash if it doesn't exist. If a field is already expired, it will be deleted rather than expired.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        unixSeconds - The expiration time to set for the fields, as a Unix timestamp in seconds.
        fields - The fields to set expiration for.
        options - The HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions for the command, supporting only expiration conditions (NX/XX/GT/LT).
        Returns:
        Command response - An array of Boolean values, each corresponding to a field:
        • true if the expiration time was successfully set for the field.
        • false if the field does not exist or the expiration time was not set due to the condition not being met.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 // Set expiration only if fields have existing expiration
 HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options = HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions.builder()
     .onlyIfHasExpiry()
     .build();

 String[] fields = {"field1", "field2"};
 long unixTimestamp = System.currentTimeMillis() / 1000 + 3600; // 1 hour from now
 batch.hexpireat("myHash", unixTimestamp, fields, options);

      • hpexpireat

        public <ArgType> T hpexpireat​(@NonNull
                              ArgType key,
                              long unixMilliseconds,
                              @NonNull
                              @NonNull ArgType[] fields,
                              @NonNull
                              @NonNull HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options)
        Sets expiration time for hash fields, using an absolute Unix timestamp in milliseconds. HPEXPIREAT has the same effect and semantic as HEXPIREAT, but the Unix time at which the field will expire is specified in milliseconds instead of seconds. See hexpireat(Object, long, Object[], HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions) for more details.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        unixMilliseconds - The expiration time to set for the fields, as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
        fields - The fields to set expiration for.
        options - The HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions for the command, supporting only expiration conditions (NX/XX/GT/LT).
        Returns:
        Command response - An array of Boolean values, each corresponding to a field:
        • true if the expiration time was successfully set for the field.
        • false if the field does not exist or the expiration time was not set due to the condition not being met.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 // Set expiration only if new expiration is less than current
 HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions options = HashFieldExpirationConditionOptions.builder()
     .onlyIfLessThanCurrent()
     .build();

 String[] fields = {"field1", "field2"};
 long unixTimestamp = System.currentTimeMillis() + 1800000; // 30 minutes from now
 batch.hpexpireat("myHash", unixTimestamp, fields, options);

      • httl

        public <ArgType> T httl​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key,
                        @NonNull
                        @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Returns the remaining time to live of hash fields that have a timeout, in seconds.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to get the TTL for.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of Long values, each corresponding to a field:
        • TTL in seconds if the field exists and has a timeout.
        • -1 if the field exists but has no associated expire.
        • -2 if the field does not exist.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hpttl

        public <ArgType> T hpttl​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Returns the remaining time to live of hash fields that have a timeout, in milliseconds.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to get the TTL for.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of TTL values in milliseconds for the specified fields:
        • For fields with a timeout, returns the remaining TTL in milliseconds.
        • For fields that exist but have no associated expire, returns -1.
        • For fields that do not exist, returns -2.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hexpiretime

        public <ArgType> T hexpiretime​(@NonNull
                               ArgType key,
                               @NonNull
                               @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Returns the absolute Unix timestamp (in seconds) at which the given hash fields will expire.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to get the expiration timestamp for.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of expiration timestamps in seconds for the specified fields:
        • For fields with a timeout, returns the absolute Unix timestamp in seconds.
        • For fields that exist but have no associated expire, returns -1.
        • For fields that do not exist, returns -2.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hpexpiretime

        public <ArgType> T hpexpiretime​(@NonNull
                                ArgType key,
                                @NonNull
                                @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Returns the absolute Unix timestamp (in milliseconds) at which the given hash fields will expire.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to get the expiration timestamp for.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of expiration timestamps in milliseconds for the specified fields:
        • For fields with a timeout, returns the absolute Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
        • For fields that exist but have no associated expire, returns -1.
        • For fields that do not exist, returns -2.
        Since:
        Valkey 9.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hsetnx

        public <ArgType> T hsetnx​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType field,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType value)
        Sets field in the hash stored at key to value, only if field does not yet exist.
        If key does not exist, a new key holding a hash is created.
        If field already exists, this operation has no effect.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        field - The field to set the value for.
        value - The value to set.
        Returns:
        Command Response - true if the field was set, false if the field already existed and was not set.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hdel

        public <ArgType> T hdel​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key,
                        @NonNull
                        @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Removes the specified fields from the hash stored at key. Specified fields that do not exist within this hash are ignored.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields to remove from the hash stored at key.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of fields that were removed from the hash, not including specified but non-existing fields.
        If key does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash and it returns 0.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hlen

        public <ArgType> T hlen​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key)
        Returns the number of fields contained in the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of fields in the hash, or 0 when the key does not exist.
        If key holds a value that is not a hash, an error is returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hvals

        public <ArgType> T hvals​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key)
        Returns all values in the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of values in the hash, or an empty array when the key does not exist.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hmget

        public <ArgType> T hmget​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ArgType[] fields)
        Returns the values associated with the specified fields in the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        fields - The fields in the hash stored at key to retrieve from the database.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of values associated with the given fields, in the same order as they are requested.
        For every field that does not exist in the hash, a null value is returned.
        If key does not exist, it is treated as an empty hash, and it returns an array of null values.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hexists

        public <ArgType> T hexists​(@NonNull
                           ArgType key,
                           @NonNull
                           ArgType field)
        Returns if field is an existing field in the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        field - The field to check in the hash stored at key.
        Returns:
        Command Response - true if the hash contains the specified field. If the hash does not contain the field, or if the key does not exist, it returns false .
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hgetall

        public <ArgType> T hgetall​(@NonNull
                           ArgType key)
        Returns all fields and values of the hash stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map of fields and their values stored in the hash. Every field name in the map is associated with its corresponding value.
        If key does not exist, it returns an empty map.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hincrBy

        public <ArgType> T hincrBy​(@NonNull
                           ArgType key,
                           @NonNull
                           ArgType field,
                           long amount)
        Increments the number stored at field in the hash stored at key by increment. By using a negative increment value, the value stored at field in the hash stored at key is decremented. If field or key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        field - The field in the hash stored at key to increment or decrement its value.
        amount - The amount by which to increment or decrement the field's value. Use a negative value to decrement.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of field in the hash stored at key after the increment or decrement.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hincrByFloat

        public <ArgType> T hincrByFloat​(@NonNull
                                ArgType key,
                                @NonNull
                                ArgType field,
                                double amount)
        Increments the string representing a floating point number stored at field in the hash stored at key by increment. By using a negative increment value, the value stored at field in the hash stored at key is decremented. If field or key does not exist, it is set to 0 before performing the operation.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        field - The field in the hash stored at key to increment or decrement its value.
