        Command Response - A Map with two keys:
        • running_script with information about the running script.
        • engines with information about available engines and their stats.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • functionDump

        public T functionDump()
        Returns the serialized payload of all loaded libraries. The command will be routed to a random node.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The serialized payload of all loaded libraries.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • functionRestore

        public T functionRestore​(@NonNull
                         @lombok.NonNull byte[] payload)
        Restores libraries from the serialized payload returned by functionDump(). The command will be routed to all primary nodes.
        Parameters:
        payload - The serialized data from functionDump().
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • functionRestore

        public T functionRestore​(@NonNull
                         @lombok.NonNull byte[] payload,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull FunctionRestorePolicy policy)
        Restores libraries from the serialized payload returned by functionDump(). The command will be routed to all primary nodes.
        Parameters:
        payload - The serialized data from functionDump().
        policy - A policy for handling existing libraries.
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • setbit

        public <ArgType> T setbit​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long offset,
                          long value)
        Sets or clears the bit at offset in the string value stored at key. The offset is a zero-based index, with 0 being the first element of the list, 1 being the next element, and so on. The offset must be less than 2^32 and greater than or equal to 0. If a key is non-existent then the bit at offset is set to value and the preceding bits are set to 0.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        offset - The index of the bit to be set.
        value - The bit value to set at offset. The value must be 0 or 1.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The bit value that was previously stored at offset.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • getbit

        public <ArgType> T getbit​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long offset)
        Returns the bit value at offset in the string value stored at key. offset should be greater than or equal to zero.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        offset - The index of the bit to return.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The bit at offset of the string. Returns zero if the key is empty or if the positive offset exceeds the length of the string.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • blmpop

        public <ArgType> T blmpop​(@NonNull
                          @NonNull ArgType[] keys,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull ListDirection direction,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull java.lang.Long count,
                          double timeout)
        Blocks the connection until it pops one or more elements from the first non-empty list from the provided keys. BLMPOP is the blocking variant of #lmpop(ArgType[], ListDirection, Long).
        Parameters:
        keys - The list of provided key names.
        direction - The direction based on which elements are popped from - see ListDirection.
        count - The maximum number of popped elements.
        timeout - The number of seconds to wait for a blocking operation to complete. A value of 0 will block indefinitely.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map of key names arrays of popped elements.
        If no member could be popped and the timeout expired, returns null.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • blmpop

        public <ArgType> T blmpop​(@NonNull
                          @NonNull ArgType[] keys,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull ListDirection direction,
                          double timeout)
        Blocks the connection until it pops one element from the first non-empty list from the provided keys. BLMPOP is the blocking variant of #lmpop(ArgType[], ListDirection).
        Parameters:
        keys - The list of provided key names.
        direction - The direction based on which elements are popped from - see ListDirection.
        timeout - The number of seconds to wait for a blocking operation to complete. A value of 0 will block indefinitely.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map of key names arrays of popped elements.
        If no member could be popped and the timeout expired, returns null.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitpos

        public <ArgType> T bitpos​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long bit)
        Returns the position of the first bit matching the given bit value.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        bit - The bit value to match. The value must be 0 or 1.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The position of the first occurrence matching bit in the binary value of the string held at key. If bit is not found, a -1 is returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitpos

        public <ArgType> T bitpos​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long bit,
                          long start)
        Returns the position of the first bit matching the given bit value. The offset start is a zero-based index, with 0 being the first byte of the list, 1 being the next byte and so on. These offsets can also be negative numbers indicating offsets starting at the end of the list, with -1 being the last byte of the list, -2 being the penultimate, and so on.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        bit - The bit value to match. The value must be 0 or 1.
        start - The starting offset.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The position of the first occurrence beginning at the start offset of the bit in the binary value of the string held at key . If bit is not found, a -1 is returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitpos

        public <ArgType> T bitpos​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long bit,
                          long start,
                          long end)
        Returns the position of the first bit matching the given bit value. The offsets start and end are zero-based indexes, with 0 being the first byte of the list, 1 being the next byte and so on. These offsets can also be negative numbers indicating offsets starting at the end of the list, with -1 being the last byte of the list, -2 being the penultimate, and so on.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        bit - The bit value to match. The value must be 0 or 1.
        start - The starting offset.
        end - The ending offset.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The position of the first occurrence from the start to the end offsets of the bit in the binary value of the string held at key. If bit is not found, a -1 is returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitpos

        public <ArgType> T bitpos​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key,
                          long bit,
                          long start,
                          long end,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull BitmapIndexType offsetType)
        Returns the position of the first bit matching the given bit value. The offset offsetType specifies whether the offset is a BIT or BYTE. If BIT is specified, start==0 and end==2 means to look at the first three bits. If BYTE is specified, start==0 and end==2 means to look at the first three bytes The offsets are zero-based indexes, with 0 being the first element of the list, 1 being the next, and so on. These offsets can also be negative numbers indicating offsets starting at the end of the list, with -1 being the last element of the list, -2 being the penultimate, and so on.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        bit - The bit value to match. The value must be 0 or 1.
        start - The starting offset.
        end - The ending offset.
        offsetType - The index offset type. Could be either BitmapIndexType.BIT or BitmapIndexType.BYTE.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The position of the first occurrence from the start to the end offsets of the bit in the binary value of the string held at key. If bit is not found, a -1 is returned.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitop

        public <ArgType> T bitop​(@NonNull
                         @NonNull BitwiseOperation bitwiseOperation,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType destination,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
        Perform a bitwise operation between multiple keys (containing string values) and store the result in the destination.
        Parameters:
        bitwiseOperation - The bitwise operation to perform.
        destination - The key that will store the resulting string.
        keys - The list of keys to perform the bitwise operation on.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The size of the string stored in destination.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lmpop

        public <ArgType> T lmpop​(@NonNull
                         @NonNull ArgType[] keys,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ListDirection direction,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull java.lang.Long count)
        Pops one or more elements from the first non-empty list from the provided keys .
        Parameters:
        keys - An array of keys to lists.
        direction - The direction based on which elements are popped from - see ListDirection.
        count - The maximum number of popped elements.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map of key name mapped arrays of popped elements. If no member could be popped, returns null.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lmpop

        public <ArgType> T lmpop​(@NonNull
                         @NonNull ArgType[] keys,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ListDirection direction)
        Pops one element from the first non-empty list from the provided keys.
        Parameters:
        keys - An array of keys to lists.
        direction - The direction based on which elements are popped from - see ListDirection.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map of key name mapped array of the popped element. If no member could be popped, returns null.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lset

        public <ArgType> T lset​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key,
                        long index,
                        @NonNull
                        ArgType element)
        Sets the list element at index to element.
        The index is zero-based, so 0 means the first element, 1 the second element and so on. Negative indices can be used to designate elements starting at the tail of the list. Here, -1 means the last element, -2 means the penultimate and so forth.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list.
        index - The index of the element in the list to be set.
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lmove

        public <ArgType> T lmove​(@NonNull
                         ArgType source,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType destination,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ListDirection whereFrom,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ListDirection whereTo)
        Atomically pops and removes the left/right-most element to the list stored at source depending on whereFrom, and pushes the element at the first/last element of the list stored at destination depending on whereFrom.
        Parameters:
        source - The key to the source list.
        destination - The key to the destination list.
        whereFrom - The ListDirection the element should be removed from.
        whereTo - The ListDirection the element should be added to.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The popped element or null if source does not exist.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • blmove

        public <ArgType> T blmove​(@NonNull
                          ArgType source,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType destination,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull ListDirection whereFrom,
                          @NonNull
                          @NonNull ListDirection whereTo,
                          double timeout)
        Blocks the connection until it atomically pops and removes the left/right-most element to the list stored at source depending on whereFrom, and pushes the element at the first/last element of the list stored at destination depending on whereFrom.
        BLMove is the blocking variant of #lmove(ArgType, ArgType, ListDirection, ListDirection).
        Parameters:
        source - The key to the source list.
        destination - The key to the destination list.
        whereFrom - The ListDirection the element should be removed from.
        whereTo - The ListDirection the element should be added to.
        timeout - The number of seconds to wait for a blocking operation to complete. A value of 0 will block indefinitely.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The popped element or null if source does not exist or if the operation timed-out.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • srandmember

        public <ArgType> T srandmember​(@NonNull
                               ArgType key)
        Returns a random element from the set value stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key from which to retrieve the set member.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A random element from the set, or null if key does not exist.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • srandmember

        public <ArgType> T srandmember​(@NonNull
                               ArgType key,
                               long count)
        Returns random elements from the set value stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key from which to retrieve the set members.
        count - The number of elements to return.
        If count is positive, returns unique elements.
        If negative, allows for duplicates.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of elements from the set, or an empty array if key does not exist.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • spop

        public <ArgType> T spop​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key)
        Removes and returns one random member from the set stored at key.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the set.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The value of the popped member.
        If key does not exist, null will be returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • spopCount

        public <ArgType> T spopCount​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             long count)
        Removes and returns up to count random members from the set stored at key , depending on the set's length.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the set.
        count - The count of the elements to pop from the set.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Set of popped elements will be returned depending on the set's length.
        If key does not exist, an empty Set will be returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitfield

        public <ArgType> T bitfield​(@NonNull
                            ArgType key,
                            @NonNull
                            @NonNull BitFieldOptions.BitFieldSubCommands[] subCommands)
        Reads or modifies the array of bits representing the string that is held at key based on the specified subCommands.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        subCommands - The subCommands to be performed on the binary value of the string at key, which could be any of the following:
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldGet.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldSet.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldIncrby.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldOverflow.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of results from the executed subcommands.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldGet returns the value in BitFieldOptions.Offset or BitFieldOptions.OffsetMultiplier.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldSet returns the old value in BitFieldOptions.Offset or BitFieldOptions.OffsetMultiplier.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldIncrby returns the new value in BitFieldOptions.Offset or BitFieldOptions.OffsetMultiplier.
        • BitFieldOptions.BitFieldOverflow determines the behaviour of SET and INCRBY when an overflow occurs. OVERFLOW does not return a value and does not contribute a value to the array response.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • bitfieldReadOnly

        public <ArgType> T bitfieldReadOnly​(@NonNull
                                    ArgType key,
                                    @NonNull
                                    @NonNull BitFieldOptions.BitFieldReadOnlySubCommands[] subCommands)
        Reads the array of bits representing the string that is held at key based on the specified subCommands.
        This command is routed depending on the client's ReadFrom strategy.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the string.
        subCommands - The GET subCommands to be performed.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of results from the GET subcommands.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.0 and above
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • functionFlush

        public T functionFlush()
        Deletes all function libraries.
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • functionFlush

        public T functionFlush​(@NonNull
                       @NonNull FlushMode mode)
        Deletes all function libraries.
        Parameters:
        mode - The flushing mode, could be either FlushMode.SYNC or FlushMode.ASYNC.
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • functionDelete

        public <ArgType> T functionDelete​(@NonNull
                                  ArgType libName)
        Deletes a library and all its functions.
        Parameters:
        libName - The library name to delete.
        Returns:
        Command Response - OK.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lcs

        public <ArgType> T lcs​(@NonNull
                       ArgType key1,
                       @NonNull
                       ArgType key2)
        Returns all the longest common subsequences combined between strings stored at key1 and key2.
        Parameters:
        key1 - The key that stores the first string.
        key2 - The key that stores the second string.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A String containing all the longest common subsequences combined between the 2 strings. An empty String/GlideString is returned if the keys do not exist or have no common subsequences.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lcsLen

        public <ArgType> T lcsLen​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key1,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType key2)
        Returns the total length of all the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
        Parameters:
        key1 - The key that stores the first string.
        key2 - The key that stores the second string.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The total length of all the longest common subsequences between the 2 strings.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • publish

        public <ArgType> T publish​(@NonNull
                           ArgType message,
                           @NonNull
                           ArgType channel)
        Publishes message on pubsub channel.
        Parameters:
        message - The message to publish.
        channel - The channel to publish the message on.
        Returns:
        Command response - The number of clients that received the message.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • pubsubChannels

        public T pubsubChannels()
        Lists the currently active channels.
        Returns:
        Command response - An Array of all active channels.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • pubsubChannels

        public <ArgType> T pubsubChannels​(@NonNull
                                  ArgType pattern)
        Lists the currently active channels.
        Parameters:
        pattern - A glob-style pattern to match active channels.
        Returns:
        Command response - An Array of currently active channels matching the given pattern.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • pubsubNumPat

        public T pubsubNumPat()
        Returns the number of unique patterns that are subscribed to by clients.
        Returns:
        Command response - The number of unique patterns.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • pubsubNumSub

        public <ArgType> T pubsubNumSub​(@NonNull
                                @NonNull ArgType[] channels)
        Returns the number of subscribers (exclusive of clients subscribed to patterns) for the specified channels.
        Parameters:
        channels - The list of channels to query for the number of subscribers.
        Returns:
        Command response - A Map where keys are the channel names and values are the numbers of subscribers.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sunion

        public <ArgType> T sunion​(@NonNull
                          @NonNull ArgType[] keys)
        Gets the union of all the given sets.
        Parameters:
        keys - The keys of the sets.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Set of members which are present in at least one of the given sets. If none of the sets exist, an empty set will be returned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lcsIdx

        public <ArgType> T lcsIdx​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key1,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType key2)
        Returns the indices and length of the longest common subsequence between strings stored at key1 and key2.
        Parameters:
        key1 - The key that stores the first string.
        key2 - The key that stores the second string.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map containing the indices of the longest common subsequence between the 2 strings and the total length of all the longest common subsequences. The resulting map contains two keys, "matches" and "len":
        • "len" is mapped to the total length of the all longest common subsequences between the 2 strings stored as Long.
        • "matches" is mapped to a three dimensional Long array that stores pairs of indices that represent the location of the common subsequences in the strings held by key1 and key2.
        See example of StringBaseCommands.lcsIdx(String, String) for more details.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lcsIdx

        public <ArgType> T lcsIdx​(@NonNull
                          ArgType key1,
                          @NonNull
                          ArgType key2,
                          long minMatchLen)
        Returns the indices and the total length of all the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
        Parameters:
        key1 - The key that stores the first string.
        key2 - The key that stores the second string.
        minMatchLen - The minimum length of matches to include in the result.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map containing the indices of the longest common subsequence between the 2 strings and the total length of all the longest common subsequences. The resulting map contains two keys, "matches" and "len":
        • "len" is mapped to the total length of the all longest common subsequences between the 2 strings stored as Long. This value doesn't count towards the minMatchLen filter.
        • "matches" is mapped to a three dimensional Long array that stores pairs of indices that represent the location of the common subsequences in the strings held by key1 and key2.
        See example of StringBaseCommands.lcsIdx(String, String, long) for more details.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lcsIdxWithMatchLen

        public <ArgType> T lcsIdxWithMatchLen​(@NonNull
                                      ArgType key1,
                                      @NonNull
                                      ArgType key2)
        Returns the indices and lengths of the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
        Parameters:
        key1 - The key that stores the first string.
        key2 - The key that stores the second string.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map containing the indices of the longest common subsequence between the 2 strings and the lengths of the longest common subsequences. The resulting map contains two keys, "matches" and "len":
        • "len" is mapped to the total length of the all longest common subsequences between the 2 strings stored as Long.
        • "matches" is mapped to a three dimensional array that stores pairs of indices that represent the location of the common subsequences in the strings held by key1 and key2 and the match length.
        See example of StringBaseCommands.lcsIdxWithMatchLen(String, String) for more details.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • lcsIdxWithMatchLen

        public <ArgType> T lcsIdxWithMatchLen​(@NonNull
                                      ArgType key1,
                                      @NonNull
                                      ArgType key2,
                                      long minMatchLen)
        Returns the indices and lengths of the longest common subsequences between strings stored at key1 and key2.
        Parameters:
        key1 - The key that stores the first string.
        key2 - The key that stores the second string.
        minMatchLen - The minimum length of matches to include in the result.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A Map containing the indices of the longest common subsequence between the 2 strings and the lengths of the longest common subsequences. The resulting map contains two keys, "matches" and "len":
        • "len" is mapped to the total length of the all longest common subsequences between the 2 strings stored as Long.
        • "matches" is mapped to a three dimensional array that stores pairs of indices that represent the location of the common subsequences in the strings held by key1 and key2 and the match length.
        See example of StringBaseCommands.lcsIdxWithMatchLen(String, String, long) for more details.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sort

        public <ArgType> T sort​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key)
        Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result.
        The sort command can be used to sort elements based on different criteria and apply transformations on sorted elements.
        To store the result into a new key, see #sortStore(ArgType, ArgType).
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list, set, or sorted set to be sorted.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of sorted elements.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sort

        public <ArgType> T sort​(@NonNull
                        ArgType key,
                        @NonNull
                        @NonNull SortOptions sortOptions)
        Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result. The sort command can be used to sort elements based on different criteria and apply transformations on sorted elements.
        To store the result into a new key, see #sortStore(ArgType, ArgType, SortOptions).
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list, set, or sorted set to be sorted.
        sortOptions - The SortOptions.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of sorted elements.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sortReadOnly

        public <ArgType> T sortReadOnly​(@NonNull
                                ArgType key)
        Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result.
        The sortReadOnly command can be used to sort elements based on different criteria and apply transformations on sorted elements.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list, set, or sorted set to be sorted.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of sorted elements.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sortReadOnly

        public <ArgType> T sortReadOnly​(@NonNull
                                ArgType key,
                                @NonNull
                                @NonNull SortOptions sortOptions)
        Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and returns the result. The sortReadOnly command can be used to sort elements based on different criteria and apply transformations on sorted elements.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list, set, or sorted set to be sorted.
        sortOptions - The SortOptions.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of sorted elements.
        Since:
        Valkey 7.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sortStore

        public <ArgType> T sortStore​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             @NonNull
                             ArgType destination)
        Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and stores the result in destination. The sort command can be used to sort elements based on different criteria, apply transformations on sorted elements, and store the result in a new key.
        To get the sort result without storing it into a key, see #sort(ArgType) or #sortReadOnly(ArgType).
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list, set, or sorted set to be sorted.
        destination - The key where the sorted result will be stored.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of elements in the sorted key stored at destination .
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sortStore

        public <ArgType> T sortStore​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             @NonNull
                             ArgType destination,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull SortOptions sortOptions)
        Sorts the elements in the list, set, or sorted set at key and stores the result in destination. The sort command can be used to sort elements based on different criteria, apply transformations on sorted elements, and store the result in a new key.
        To get the sort result without storing it into a key, see #sort(ArgType, SortOptions).
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the list, set, or sorted set to be sorted.
        sortOptions - The SortOptions.
        destination - The key where the sorted result will be stored.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of elements in the sorted key stored at destination .
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • geosearch

        public <ArgType> T geosearch​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy)
        Returns the members of a sorted set populated with geospatial information using #geoadd(ArgType, Map), which are within the borders of the area specified by a given shape.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of matched member names.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearch

        public <ArgType> T geosearch​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchResultOptions resultOptions)
        Returns the members of a sorted set populated with geospatial information using #geoadd(ArgType, Map), which are within the borders of the area specified by a given shape.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        resultOptions - Optional inputs for sorting/limiting the results. See - GeoSearchResultOptions
        Returns:
        Command Response - An array of matched member names.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearch

        public <ArgType> T geosearch​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchOptions options)
        Returns the members of a sorted set populated with geospatial information using #geoadd(ArgType, Map), which are within the borders of the area specified by a given shape.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        options - The optional inputs to request additional information.
        Returns:
        Command Response - A 2D array of arrays where each sub-array represents a single item in the following order:
        • The member (location) name.
        • The distance from the center as a Double, in the same unit specified for searchBy.
        • The geohash of the location as a Long.
        • The coordinates as a two item array of Double.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearch

        public <ArgType> T geosearch​(@NonNull
                             ArgType key,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchOptions options,
                             @NonNull
                             @NonNull GeoSearchResultOptions resultOptions)
        Returns the members of a sorted set populated with geospatial information using #geoadd(ArgType, Map), which are within the borders of the area specified by a given shape.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        options - The optional inputs to request additional information.
        resultOptions - Optional inputs for sorting/limiting the results. See - GeoSearchResultOptions
        Returns:
        Command Response - A 2D array of arrays where each sub-array represents a single item in the following order:
        • The member (location) name.
        • The distance from the center as a Double, in the same unit specified for searchBy.
        • The geohash of the location as a Long.
        • The coordinates as a two item array of Double.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearchstore

        public <ArgType> T geosearchstore​(@NonNull
                                  ArgType destination,
                                  @NonNull
                                  ArgType source,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy)
        Searches for members in a sorted set stored at source representing geospatial data within a circular or rectangular area and stores the result in destination. If destination already exists, it is overwritten. Otherwise, a new sorted set will be created. To get the result directly, see `#geosearch(ArgType, SearchOrigin, GeoSearchShape).
        Parameters:
        destination - The key of the destination sorted set.
        source - The key of the source sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of elements in the resulting set.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearchstore

        public <ArgType> T geosearchstore​(@NonNull
                                  ArgType destination,
                                  @NonNull
                                  ArgType source,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchResultOptions resultOptions)
        Searches for members in a sorted set stored at source representing geospatial data within a circular or rectangular area and stores the result in destination. If destination already exists, it is overwritten. Otherwise, a new sorted set will be created. To get the result directly, see `#geosearch(ArgType, SearchOrigin, GeoSearchShape, GeoSearchResultOptions).
        Parameters:
        destination - The key of the destination sorted set.
        source - The key of the source sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        resultOptions - Optional inputs for sorting/limiting the results. See - GeoSearchResultOptions
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of elements in the resulting set.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearchstore

        public <ArgType> T geosearchstore​(@NonNull
                                  ArgType destination,
                                  @NonNull
                                  ArgType source,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchStoreOptions options)
        Searches for members in a sorted set stored at source representing geospatial data within a circular or rectangular area and stores the result in destination. If destination already exists, it is overwritten. Otherwise, a new sorted set will be created. To get the result directly, see `#geosearch(ArgType, SearchOrigin, GeoSearchShape, GeoSearchOptions).
        Parameters:
        destination - The key of the destination sorted set.
        source - The key of the source sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        options - The optional inputs to request additional information.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of elements in the resulting set.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • geosearchstore

        public <ArgType> T geosearchstore​(@NonNull
                                  ArgType destination,
                                  @NonNull
                                  ArgType source,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchOrigin.SearchOrigin searchFrom,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchShape searchBy,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchStoreOptions options,
                                  @NonNull
                                  @NonNull GeoSearchResultOptions resultOptions)
        Searches for members in a sorted set stored at source representing geospatial data within a circular or rectangular area and stores the result in destination. If destination already exists, it is overwritten. Otherwise, a new sorted set will be created. To get the result directly, see `#geosearch(ArgType, SearchOrigin, GeoSearchShape, GeoSearchOptions, GeoSearchResultOptions).
        Parameters:
        destination - The key of the destination sorted set.
        source - The key of the source sorted set.
        searchFrom - The query's center point options, could be one of:
        • GeoSearchOrigin.MemberOrigin to use the position of the given existing member in the sorted set.
        • GeoSearchOrigin.CoordOrigin to use the given longitude and latitude coordinates.
        searchBy - The query's shape options:
        • GeoSearchShape(double, GeoUnit) to search inside circular area according to given radius.
        • GeoSearchShape(double, double, GeoUnit) to search inside an axis-aligned rectangle, determined by height and width.
        options - The optional inputs to request additional information.
        resultOptions - Optional inputs for sorting/limiting the results. See - GeoSearchResultOptions
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of elements in the resulting set.
        Since:
        Valkey 6.2.0 and above.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for more details.

      • sscan

        public <ArgType> T sscan​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType cursor)
        Iterates incrementally over a set.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the set.
        cursor - The cursor that points to the next iteration of results. A value of "0" indicates the start of the search.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of Objects. The first element is always the cursor for the next iteration of results. "0" will be the cursor returned on the last iteration of the set. The second element is always an Array of the subset of the set held in key.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • sscan

        public <ArgType> T sscan​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType cursor,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull SScanOptions sScanOptions)
        Iterates incrementally over a set.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the set.
        cursor - The cursor that points to the next iteration of results. A value of "0" indicates the start of the search.
        sScanOptions - The SScanOptions.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of Objects. The first element is always the cursor for the next iteration of results. "0" will be the cursor returned on the last iteration of the set. The second element is always an Array of the subset of the set held in key.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • zscan

        public <ArgType> T zscan​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType cursor)
        Iterates incrementally over a sorted set.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the sorted set.
        cursor - The cursor that points to the next iteration of results. A value of "0" indicates the start of the search.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of Objects. The first element is always the cursor for the next iteration of results. "0" will be the cursor returned on the last iteration of the sorted set. The second element is always an Array of the subset of the sorted set held in key . The array in the second element is always a flattened series of String pairs, where the value is at even indices and the score is at odd indices.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • zscan

        public <ArgType> T zscan​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType cursor,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull ZScanOptions zScanOptions)
        Iterates incrementally over a sorted set.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the sorted set.
        cursor - The cursor that points to the next iteration of results. A value of "0" indicates the start of the search.
        zScanOptions - The ZScanOptions.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of Objects. The first element is always the cursor for the next iteration of results. "0" will be the cursor returned on the last iteration of the sorted set. The second element is always an Array of the subset of the sorted set held in key . The array in the second element is a flattened series of String pairs, where the value is at even indices and the score is at odd indices. If ZScanOptions.ZScanOptionsBuilder.noScores(boolean) is to true, the second element will only contain the members without scores.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hscan

        public <ArgType> T hscan​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType cursor)
        Iterates fields of Hash types and their associated values.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        cursor - The cursor that points to the next iteration of results. A value of "0" indicates the start of the search.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of Objects. The first element is always the cursor for the next iteration of results. "0" will be the cursor returned on the last iteration of the result. The second element is always an Array of the subset of the hash held in key. The array in the second element is always a flattened series of String pairs, where the key is at even indices and the value is at odd indices.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • hscan

        public <ArgType> T hscan​(@NonNull
                         ArgType key,
                         @NonNull
                         ArgType cursor,
                         @NonNull
                         @NonNull HScanOptions hScanOptions)
        Iterates fields of Hash types and their associated values.
        Parameters:
        key - The key of the hash.
        cursor - The cursor that points to the next iteration of results. A value of "0" indicates the start of the search.
        hScanOptions - The HScanOptions.
        Returns:
        Command Response - An Array of Objects. The first element is always the cursor for the next iteration of results. "0" will be the cursor returned on the last iteration of the result. The second element is always an Array of the subset of the hash held in key. The array in the second element is a flattened series of String pairs, where the key is at even indices and the value is at odd indices. If HScanOptions.HScanOptionsBuilder.noValues(boolean) is set to true, the second element will only contain the fields without the values.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • wait

        public T wait​(long numReplicas,
              long timeout)
        Returns the number of replicas that acknowledged the write commands sent by the current client before this command, both in the case where the specified number of replicas are reached, or when the timeout is reached.
        Parameters:
        numReplicas - The number of replicas to reach.
        timeout - The timeout value specified in milliseconds.
        Returns:
        Command Response - The number of replicas reached by all the writes performed in the context of the current connection.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.

      • buildCommand

        protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command buildCommand​(CommandRequestOuterClass.RequestType requestType)
        Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command object for given command and arguments.

      • buildCommand

        protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command buildCommand​(CommandRequestOuterClass.RequestType requestType,
                                                        CommandRequestOuterClass.Command.ArgsArray args)
        Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command object for given command and arguments.

      • buildCommand

        protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command buildCommand​(CommandRequestOuterClass.RequestType requestType,
                                                        ArgsBuilder argsBuilder)
        Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command object for given command and arguments.

      • emptyArgs

        protected CommandRequestOuterClass.Command.ArgsArray emptyArgs()
        Build protobuf CommandRequestOuterClass.Command.ArgsArray object for empty arguments.

      • createArray

        @SafeVarargs
protected final <ArgType> ArgType[] createArray​(ArgType... args)
        Helper function for creating generic type ("ArgType") array

      • isBinaryOutput

        public boolean isBinaryOutput()
        Flag whether batch commands may return binary data.
        If set to true, all commands in this batch return GlideString instead of String.

      • getProtobufBatch

        public CommandRequestOuterClass.Batch.Builder getProtobufBatch()
        Command class to send a single request to Valkey.
